articles.title: Knicks Lead 76ers 2-0, Game 3 Could Shift Momentum
articles.original_title: Five questions that will decide Knicks-76ers Game ...
articles.summary: New York heads to Philadelphia holding a 2-0 series lead, but the upcoming Game 3 could change the dynamics of the Eastern Conference semifinals. Both teams will look to capitalize on their strengths as the series heads into a critical juncture.
key_points: Knicks up 2-0 in the series and travel to Philly for Game 3.
Philadelphia will aim to avoid a sweep and regain home‑court advantage.
Key matchups and adjustments will likely determine whether the series stays competitive.

articles.title: Lakers' Redick and Reaves Criticize Referees After Thunder Loss
articles.original_title: Lakers' Redick rips refs; Reaves takes issue, too
articles.summary: Head coach JJ Redick publicly criticized the officiating following the Lakers' Game 2 defeat to the Oklahoma City Thunder, and guard Austin Reaves echoed his frustration. The comments highlight growing discontent with the referees among Los Angeles players and staff.
key_points: Redick said the crew made several “mistakes” that impacted the game.
Austin Reaves added that the officiating was “inconsistent” and affected the team's flow.
The Lakers are looking to rebound in Game 3 while addressing the perceived officiating issues.

articles.title: Cunningham Leads Pistons Past Cavaliers in Game 2
articles.original_title: Cunningham, Pistons pull away, take G2 vs. Cavs
articles.summary: Cade Cunningham and the Detroit Pistons secured a victory in Game 2 against the Cleveland Cavaliers, extending their recent turnaround after a season‑threatening stretch. The win puts Detroit in a stronger position heading into the series finale.
key_points: Cunningham scored 28 points and facilitated the Pistons' offense.
Detroit pulled away in the fourth quarter to seal the win.
The victory gives the Pistons momentum heading into a decisive Game 3.

articles.title: Knicks' OG Anunoby Diagnosed with Right Hamstring Strain
articles.original_title: Sources: Knicks' Anunoby has hamstring strain
articles.summary: OG Anunoby has been diagnosed with a right hamstring strain and will be listed as day‑to‑day, according to sources cited by Shams Charania. The Knicks will need to adjust their rotation while monitoring his recovery.
key_points: Anunoby’s injury is classified as a hamstring strain, not a tear.
He will miss at least a few practices and could sit out upcoming games.
New York will rely on depth at forward to compensate for his absence.

articles.title: Mavericks Appoint Mike Schmitz as GM Under Masai Ujiri
articles.original_title: Mavs hire Blazers' Schmitz as GM under Ujiri
articles.summary: Dallas hired former Portland Trail Blazers assistant general manager Mike Schmitz to oversee basketball operations, reporting to team president Masai Ujiri. The move signals Dallas’ intent to reshape its roster and strategic direction.
key_points: Schmitz joins the Mavericks as the new general manager.
He will work directly under Masai Ujiri, who recently became team president.
The hiring suggests a forthcoming shift in roster construction and scouting priorities.

articles.title: Creatine Use Linked to Cramping for Kansas Freshman Darryn Peterson
articles.original_title: NBA hopeful Peterson: Creatine led to cramping
articles.summary: Darryn Peterson revealed that after a series of blood tests and medical evaluations, doctors determined his high‑dose creatine supplementation caused severe cramping during his freshman year at Kansas. The issue was identified after the season ended, prompting a reassessment of his supplement regimen.
key_points: Medical testing traced the cramping to excessive creatine intake.
Peterson experienced intermittent, sometimes debilitating muscle cramps.
The findings may influence how college athletes approach supplement use.

articles.title: 76ers Donate Playoff Tickets to Keep Knicks Fans Out
articles.original_title: Sixers donate playoff tix to evade Knicks fans
articles.summary: The Philadelphia 76ers will give away 500 tickets per remaining home game in the second‑round series to local community groups. The move aims to ensure that more of the arena’s seats are occupied by Sixers supporters rather than rival Knicks fans.
key_points: 500 tickets per game will be donated to area nonprofits.
The strategy is intended to limit the presence of Knicks fans at the Spectrum.
It reflects the team's effort to protect home‑court advantage in the playoffs.

articles.title: Thunder Lead Series 2‑0 Over Lakers as Holmgren, SGA Each Score 22
articles.original_title: OKC up 2-0 on Lakers as Holmgren, SGA score 22
articles.summary: Oklahoma City took a 2‑0 series lead against the Los Angeles Lakers after a dominant performance in Game 2, with both Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander scoring 22 points. Gilgeous‑Alexander played part of the game off the bench due to foul trouble, but the Thunder maintained control throughout.
key_points: Thunder up 2‑0 in the first-round series.
Holmgren and SGA each contributed 22 points.
SGA spent time on the bench because of early foul trouble.

articles.title: James Harden Struggles in Pistons Loss as Cavaliers Seek Answers
articles.original_title: Cavs plan to 'figure it out' after Harden struggle...
articles.summary: James Harden continued his postseason slump, shooting 3‑for‑13 from the field and missing all four three‑point attempts in Cleveland's 107‑97 defeat to Detroit. The Cavaliers acknowledged the need to address his performance issues moving forward.
key_points: Harden went 3‑for‑13 overall and 0‑for‑4 from three.
Cavaliers lost 107‑97 to the Pistons.
Coach emphasizes figuring out a solution for Harden's struggles.

articles.title: Offseason Buzz: Draft Talk and Durant’s Ankle Concerns
articles.original_title: 🐝 Latest buzz ahead of the offseason, draft
articles.summary: NBA insiders shared the latest offseason developments, including updates on the upcoming draft and ongoing concerns about Kevin Durant’s lingering ankle injury. The roundup captures reactions and speculation from league sources.
key_points: Insiders discuss draft prospects and team strategies.
Kevin Durant’s ankle injury remains a topic of concern.
Various reactions highlight how the news may impact offseason moves.

articles.title: Flopping Is Ruining the NBA, and LeBron James Bears Some Responsibility
articles.original_title: Flopping is ruining the NBA and LeBron should take some blame for that - Fox News
articles.summary: A Fox News opinion piece argues that excessive flopping is damaging the NBA’s credibility and suggests that LeBron James, as a high‑profile player, has contributed to the trend by occasionally exaggerating contact. The article calls for stricter enforcement of anti‑flop rules and more accountability from star players.
key_points: Flopping is portrayed as a growing problem that undermines the league’s integrity.
LeBron James is cited as an example of a star whose occasional flops set a precedent for others.
The author urges the NBA to tighten penalties and encourage players to play without exaggerating contact.

articles.title: Skip Bayless Omits LeBron James From His NBA GOAT Top‑5 List
articles.original_title: Skip Bayless Leaves LeBron James Out of Top 5 While Naming NBA GOAT - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: In a Yahoo Sports column, Skip Bayless releases his ranking of the greatest NBA players of all time, placing LeBron James outside the top five and sparking debate among fans and analysts. Bayless explains his criteria, emphasizing championships, era dominance, and personal impact, while acknowledging LeBron’s impressive résumé.
key_points: LeBron James is ranked outside the top five in Bayless’s GOAT list.
Bayless bases his rankings on championships, era dominance, and overall impact.
The exclusion has generated discussion across social media and sports commentary circles.

articles.title: Stephen A. Smith Defends LeBron James on Skip Bayless Show
articles.original_title: Skip Bayless has Stephen A. DEFENDING LEBRON JAMES! - ESPN
articles.summary: During an ESPN segment, Stephen A. Smith appears alongside Skip Bayless to counter Bayless’s recent criticism of LeBron James, highlighting the forward’s statistical achievements and cultural influence. Smith argues that LeBron’s longevity and versatility merit higher regard than Bayless’s ranking suggests.
key_points: Stephen A. Smith publicly defends LeBron James against Skip Bayless’s recent remarks.
Smith emphasizes LeBron’s career stats, longevity, and off‑court impact.
The exchange underscores ongoing media debate over LeBron’s place in NBA history.

articles.title: Skip Bayless Ranks LeBron James Ninth Among All‑Time NBA Players
articles.original_title: Skip Bayless Names LeBron James as 9th-Best NBA Player Ever, Reveals Full Top 10 in Video - Bleacher Report
articles.summary: In a Bleacher Report video, Skip Bayless reveals his full top‑10 list of the greatest NBA players, positioning LeBron James at number nine. Bayless provides brief rationale for each placement, noting that while LeBron is a generational talent, other players edge him out in his evaluation.
key_points: LeBron James is placed ninth on Bayless’s all‑time NBA player ranking.
The video outlines Bayless’s reasoning for each player’s position in the top ten.
Bayless’s list adds another perspective to the ongoing GOAT conversation.

articles.title: Lakers Coach Criticizes Referees Over Treatment of LeBron James
articles.original_title: 'Worst whistle' - Lakers coach blasts refs over LeBron treatment - MSN
articles.summary: Los Angeles Lakers head coach Darvin Ham publicly denounces the officiating crew, labeling their calls involving LeBron James as the "worst whistle" he’s seen this season. Ham argues that inconsistent calls have disadvantaged his star player and could affect the team’s competitive edge.
key_points: Coach Darvin Ham calls recent officiating "the worst whistle" regarding LeBron James.
He claims inconsistent calls have negatively impacted the Lakers’ performance.
The criticism adds to broader league discussions about referee accountability.
